summary: Scorching second set with epic Wharf Rat and fiery Playing In The Band
blurb: The show delivers a powerhouse second set highlighted by Lazy Lightning → Supplication, an 18‑minute Wharf Rat and a soaring Playing In The Band sequence. Jerry’s birthday guitar tone and Phil’s surprise bass‑only Dark Star solo add extra sparkle. Overall the performance is tight, energetic, and full of classic ’76 jam vibes.
review: On August 2 1976 at Colt Park, the Dead turned a warm summer night into one of those hidden gems that only surface when the band clicks on all cylinders. The first set rolls through familiar terrain, but it’s the second set that truly shines: a blistering Lazy Lightning → Supplication pair launches into a Might As Well jam that feeds directly into an inspired Samson & Delilah and Candyman, before exploding into a massive Playing In The Band → Wharf Rat → Goin' Down The Road Feeling Bad → Playing In The Band suite. Jerry Garcia, celebrating his birthday, mixes his “touch of grey” tone with searing slide work, while Phil Lesh stakes his claim with a 10‑15 minute bass‑only Dark Star intro and the “Philonius Funk” that drives the extended improvisations. Keith’s keyboards weave memorable solos, Donna’s vocals soar on several numbers, and Mickey Hart’s intricate drumming (joined by Billy Kreutzmann’s solid backbeat) keeps the momentum rolling. The encore of U.S. Blues and Sugar Magnolia caps a night that feels both relaxed and electrified – a quintessential ’76 outdoor experience captured best by the Gatto audience tape.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Four of six recordings are explicitly positive, praising the second‑set jams, standout songs and band interplay; two mixed reviews note only minor technical or balance issues but still highlight strong performances. No consistent negative commentary about playing, so a moderate positive adjustment is applied.

best_recording.reason: Good‑quality audience tape with clear capture of the full show, minimal dropouts, and strong emphasis on the standout moments (Might As Well, Lazy Lightning → Supplication, Wharf Rat).

key_highlights: Epic second‑set suite: Playing In The Band → Wharf Rat → Goin' Down The Road Feeling Bad → Playing In The Band
Rare pre‑show bass‑only Dark Star solo by Phil Lesh (10–15 minutes)
Lazy Lightning → Supplication transition and an 18‑minute Wharf Rat jam
High‑energy encore featuring U.S. Blues and Sugar Magnolia
song_highlights: Goin' Down The Road Feeling Bad

band_performance.Jerry: Celebrates his birthday with a “touch of grey” guitar tone, understated early playing that erupts into fiery slide work and a scorching Wharf Rat solo; his interplay drives the extended jams.
band_performance.Phil: Delivers a surprise 10‑15 minute bass‑only Dark Star intro, solid throughout, and adds “Philonius Funk” on the long Playing In The Band improvisations.
band_performance.Bob: Vocal energy shines on Lazy Lightning → Supplication; occasional flubs (Around & Around) are outweighed by strong rhythm work and enthusiastic singing.
band_performance.Keith: Provides memorable keyboard solos in Playing In The Band, a cool bridge during Goin' Down The Road Feeling Bad, and supports the band’s harmonic depth throughout.
band_performance.Mickey: Offers intricate, “smoking” drumming especially on the sprawling Playing In The Band jam, keeping the rhythmic momentum alive.
band_performance.Billy: Delivers solid, inventive drum work that meshes with Mickey's textures, contributing to the overall driving groove of the second set.

must_listen_sequences: Goin' Down The Road Feeling Bad
